items.title: Crows defender Josh Worrell to face court over crash

children.content: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell has been reported by police and will face court over a traffic crash last week.

gallery.altText: crows players Josh worrell, text walker and others walking off field

thumbnail.altText: crows players Josh worrell, text walker and others walking off field

items.title: Flight disruptions at Adelaide Airport due to heavy fog

children.content: A number of flights have been delayed for take-off at Adelaide Airport due to heavy fog while three others have been diverted to Melbourne.

image.altText: Three Qantas flights on tarmac at Adelaide Airport with fog around.

items.title: Fertiliser company fined after hundreds of complaints about manure stench

children.content: An Adelaide Hills business that piled chicken manure, coffee grounds and other waste up to twice the allowed height has been fined by a South Australian court after more than 700 complaints about the smell.

image.altText: Piles of fertilisers and dirt in rows on a large piece of land containing several sheds on the side

items.title: Monet, Renoir, van Gogh masterpieces appear in two new blockbuster exhibitions

children.content: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

gallery.altText: Three paintings hung in a gallery with red walls.

thumbnail.altText: Three paintings hung in a gallery with red walls.

items.title: Legal action dropped against Ann Marie Smith's NDIS provider

children.content: Federal Court proceedings have been dropped against the former company responsible for caring for South Australian woman Ann Marie Smith, who died six years ago.

image.altText: A house converted into an office

items.title: Staff shortage led to woman being strangled by wheelchair belt, court hears

children.content: A woman who died after slipping from her wheelchair and being strangled by its lap belt had been left unsupervised due to a staff shortage at an Adelaide care facility, a court has heard.

image.altText: The Coroners Court sign in Adelaide.

items.title: Alleged killer's recorded prison calls played to murder trial

children.content: Recorded prison phone calls — in which the former partner of a woman confronts her alleged murderer — have been played in South Australia's Supreme Court.

image.altText: A bearded man with a dog.

items.title: 'Falling apart': Major SA highways given lowest possible safety rating

children.content: Parts of the Sturt Highway, which connects Sydney to Adelaide and provides an integral part of the country's freight network, have been given the lowest possible safety rating by the Australian Road Assessment Program (AusRAP).

image.altText: A man in a collared shirt stands arms crossed in front of a large freight truck.

items.title: Australian lamb new favourite in US as protein 'wave' creates demand

children.content: Australians may love their lamb, but it has always been a harder sell for American consumers. There are signs that is changing.

image.altText: Picture of lamb with the American flag

items.title: Bird flu detection training to be rolled out to volunteers

children.content: Training to help volunteers identify potential cases of bird flu will be rolled out in South Australia, under a partnership between conservation groups and government.

image.altText: A flock of crested terns in flight.

items.title: Ex-cop turned missing persons expert says he may know where Peter Falconio is

children.content: Jim Whitehead has helped to close some of Australia's most high-profile missing persons cases, including those of Daniel Morcombe and Allison Baden-Clay. Now he is looking to help find the remains of British backpacker Peter Falconio.

articles.title: In Barrow Creek a $5 note hangs on the pub's wall, still waiting for Peter Falconio

children.content: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend, Joanne Lees, in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

image.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.

gallery.altText: A man sitting at a table holding a large paper map in his hands.

thumbnail.altText: A man sitting at a table holding a large paper map in his hands.

items.title: 'Gatekeeping septuagenarians': 'Inappropriate' TikTok video deleted

children.content: The National Trust of SA says one of its volunteers apologised after posting a social media clip labelling a group of disaffected members "gatekeeping septuagenarians" who were running a "stuffy museum".

image.altText: A decommissioned mining site

items.title: SA doctor banned for inappropriate relationship with client

children.content: A former Adelaide doctor will not be able to reapply for registration for at least two years after he was banned for having an inappropriate relationship with his client.

image.altText: A female doctor writing in a note pad, with a stethoscope on the table.

items.title: Coroner calls for change after man died while paramedic waited outside

children.content: A paramedic who chose not to force entry to a house, despite being told a man was inside and needed medical attention, had "a flawed understanding" of his powers to enter the property, an inquest finds.

image.altText: A green and yellow ambulance driving on a road

items.title: Man's death 'not suspicious' after 'large fight' on APY Lands, police say

children.content: Police said the death of a 59-year-old man was not suspicious after a fight broke out on South Australia's APY Lands, but investigations into the disturbance are continuing.

image.altText: A drone of houses with mountain range behind them.

items.title: Man's death in Bali leaves trail of debt, scores of customers in limbo

children.content: Cameron Hughes, 39, died in a Balinese immigration detention centre last week under yet-to-be-confirmed circumstances. The ABC can reveal the Perth businessman left behind hundreds of thousands of dollars in unfinished business, with dozens of customers potentially out of pocket.

gallery.altText: Cameron speaking while standing in front of a car in his workshop.

thumbnail.altText: Cameron speaking while standing in front of a car in his workshop.

items.title: Taylor accuses PM of creating 'office inside his office' to deal with AI

children.content: Angus Taylor says Anthony Albanese is adding to bureaucracy by creating an Office of AI within his department to deal with artificial intelligence policy. Follow live.

image.altText: Angus Taylor speaks to the media.

items.title: What Australia's economy could look like under One Nation

children.content: From interest rates to policy advice from billionaire buddy Gina Rinehart, here is how One Nation's economic platform could look.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson looks to the side seriously.

items.title: Jewish student tells of her disgust at TikToks in Auschwitz car park

children.content: Paris Enten has told the antisemitism royal commission her experiences of Monash University, including during an overseas trip, caused her mental health to deteriorate.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair in a checked blazer and dark top stares off camera.

items.title: As Japan undergoes a defence shift, China warns it's 'becoming a powder keg'

children.content: While the world has been watching the rise of China, Japan has been in the midst of its biggest defence shift since World War II.

image.altText: Japanese naval officers look over a ship.

items.title: The Thai child sex exploitation scourge unfolding at bars, restaurants and hotels

children.content: Thailand is struggling against a "hidden" world of child sexual exploitation that requires action from both the tourism industry and law enforcement agencies, according to NGOs.

gallery.altText: The black silhouette of a girl, with blurred lights in the background.
Women's feet in high heels, under red lights.

thumbnail.altText: The black silhouette of a girl, with blurred lights in the background.
Women's feet in high heels, under red lights.

items.title: Batteries now cheaper than gas for peak demand power supply

children.content: Falling battery costs and a data centre-fuelled dash for gas have overturned decades of accepted wisdom: gas is no longer the cheapest way to meet peak power demand.

image.altText: Flames belch form two tall metal industrial chimneys.

items.title: Smart glasses help vision-impaired despite privacy concerns

children.content: For people who are blind, AI-powered smart glasses deliver a level of independence that was once out of reach, but the rise of wearable cameras also raises urgent questions about surveillance, consent and the law.

image.altText: A close up photo of Sam's face wearing his black AI glasses.

items.title: Tourists turn away from NT as Kakadu travellers greeted by closure signs

children.content: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

gallery.altText: View of Twin Falls at Kakadu National Park.
A large sign by the side of a road, in a bush environment, saying several national park sites as closed.
An access prohibited sign on a dirt road in a national park.

thumbnail.altText: View of Twin Falls at Kakadu National Park.
A large sign by the side of a road, in a bush environment, saying several national park sites as closed.
An access prohibited sign on a dirt road in a national park.

items.title: Quiz: Dust off your passport and join us on a trip

children.content: Which of these peaks isn't in the Himalayas? And in what country would you find the island of Rapa Nui?

image.altText: Four Easter Island Moai silhouettes in a single file line on a platform in front of an orange setting sun and cloudy sky

items.title: More than 150 miners have died on this site. Families say they need a place to mourn

children.content: For decades, families of Mount Isa miners killed on the job have waited for a lasting tribute, but after years of campaigning, some fear their loved ones will be forgotten.

image.altText: two sisters holding each other, standing in front of green palm leaves holding a black and white photo of their brother who d

items.title: Sheep industry turns to natural remedy to beat blowflies

children.content: A biopesticide using tea-tree oil in nanoparticles has been developed to keep flies off sheep.

gallery.altText: A lab slide showing fly larvae avoiding tea tree oil
A woman in a laboratory uses a syringe to test a tea tree flystrike treatment

thumbnail.altText: A lab slide showing fly larvae avoiding tea tree oil
A woman in a laboratory uses a syringe to test a tea tree flystrike treatment

items.title: 'I'm ruling it out': Taylor categorically kills off One Nation coalition talk

children.content: Coalition leader Angus Taylor has renewed his criticism of Pauline Hanson's party and categorically ruled out any alliance with it.

image.altText: A man in suit and tie smiling while sitting at a desk.

items.title: Scale of Australia's shortage of child psychiatrists revealed in new report

children.content: Experts are warning younger children are presenting with increasingly complex mental health needs as a new report predicts a nationwide shortfall of child and adolescent psychiatrists.

image.altText: A young woman with dark shoulder-length hair look straight to camera.

items.title: Spy agency warns active Russian hackers targeting Australian industry

children.content: The Australian Signals Directorate says hackers are trying to access information through network devices, such as laptops, which use old software and predictable passwords.

gallery.altText: Cybersecurity experts say the longer a breach goes undetected, the more time attackers have to steal victims' data.

thumbnail.altText: Cybersecurity experts say the longer a breach goes undetected, the more time attackers have to steal victims' data.

items.title: Suspected Widdecombe murder being treated as a targeted attack

children.content: After initially saying the alleged murder of British former politician Ann Widdecombe was not politically motivated, police now say they believe it was a targeted attack.

image.altText: An older woman wearing a light-blue rosette points as she speaks into a microphone in front of a Reform UK logo.

items.title: Woman sues ex-Catholic archbishop over alleged abuse by another priest

children.content: A Tasmanian woman is suing former Archbishop of Hobart Julian Porteous and the state of Tasmania over allegations of sexual abuse by two others when she was a child.

image.altText: Julian Porteous stands in front of a full church.

items.title: Brisbane's lower clearance rate shows a 'disconnect' between buyers and sellers

children.content: After a decades-long
real estate boom, experts say the balance of power is finally shifting from sellers to buyers.

image.altText: A young lady with shoulder-length brown hair standing in a driveway poses for camera. She is wearing a brown jacket.

items.title: NRLW players eye off longer season and full-time salaries

children.content: A $5.3 billion broadcast deal, booming participation and new contracts on the horizon have NRLW stars hopeful their league can soar to new heights.

image.altText: A Cronulla NRLW player holds the ball close as she charges between two Brisbane tacklers.

items.title: No fee, no sign-in: The art class removing 'barriers' to recovery

children.content: In regional NSW, one program shows that creativity may be the key to better mental health.

gallery.altText: A woman sits at a table drawing a picture with pencils.

thumbnail.altText: A woman sits at a table drawing a picture with pencils.
